_comment: Optional Protection panel. Copy to ~/.config/server-monitor/protection.json and fill in your machine's real invariants. Each check runs an argv (login shell, positional args): exit 0 = OK, nonzero = AT RISK. Optional 'repair' argv re-arms a failing invariant on Repair. 'note' is a hint shown on failure. The app stores no host names or tool specifics, so the real file is never committed. DEPTH MATTERS: a 'launchctl print' check only proves a job is LOADED — for a watchdog loop also assert it is RUNNING (has a pid) and its status output is FRESH; a loaded-but-dead loop is the silent failure. For a whole stack prefer ONE composite audit command (nonzero if anything is missing/stale), e.g. darkmesh's 'darkmesh-audit'.

checks.label: Network-guard stack healthy
checks.note: one composite check: every keep-alive agent loaded+running+fresh

checks.label: Transfer client bound to the live tunnel
checks.note: exit 3 = stale binding (fail-closed, not a leak)
